items.title: Stocks close higher on Big Tech gains, encouraging inflation signs
items.content: S&P 500 gained 0.38% to 7,572.40, Nasdaq +0.62% to 26,269.23, Dow +0.29% to 52,658.64. US equities finished higher on cooler inflation data and positive earnings.

items.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
items.content: CPI fell 0.4% MoM (biggest monthly decline since April 2020), annual rate 3.5% vs 3.8% expected. Core inflation flat MoM, 12-month rate at 2.6% vs 2.9% expected. Fed rate-hike odds for September fell to 63% from 75%.

items.title: US Core PCE inflation fell to 2.6% in June 2026
items.content: US core inflation (ex food & energy) dropped to 2.6% from a 7-month high of 2.9% in May, below expected 2.8%. Driven by modest easing in shelter costs (3.3% vs 3.4%).

items.title: Bank earnings takeaways: Goldman Sachs profits soar; JPMorgan beats estimates
items.content: Goldman Sachs reported $21/share vs $14.50 expected. JPMorgan $6.14 EPS vs $5.85 expected. BlackRock and Morgan Stanley also beat estimates. Bank earnings season off to a strong start.

items.title: US stocks end higher on cool inflation data, strong earnings; PayPal surges on buyout offer
items.content: Wall Street gained as softer PPI data and strong Q2 earnings boosted sentiment. PayPal jumped on reported buyout offer. Fed Beige Book showed modest growth but sticky prices. US launched fresh strikes on Iran targeting Strait of Hormuz threats.

items.title: Hong Kong Hang Seng up 0.8%, tracking Asian gains
items.content: Hang Seng index rose 0.8% on Jul 15, tracking broad Asian market gains. Healthcare and real estate stocks among biggest gainers. Mainland China stocks closed 0.2% lower at 4,786.78 after weak GDP data.

items.title: Hang Seng jumps as healthcare and biotech stocks rally despite weak China GDP
items.content: Hang Seng soared to 24,692, up 9.7% from YTD low. Rally led by healthcare and biotechnology stocks despite China posting slowest quarterly GDP growth since 2022, missing expectations.

items.title: Micron drops 8% on China competition fears, dragging Intel AMD Marvell
items.content: A report on Chinese memory chip maker CXMT's massive IPO plans sent Micron tumbling 8%, pulling Intel, AMD, and Marvell down with it on fears of increased competition from Chinese semiconductor players.

items.title: Chipmaker CXMT eyes $9.8B in blockbuster China IPO
items.content: ChangXin Memory Technologies (CXMT) seeks to raise up to $9.8 billion in Shanghai IPO. Would be China's largest mainland IPO since 2010 and biggest ever mainland tech share sale. Aims to rival Micron, Samsung, SK hynix in AI memory chips.

items.title: How the US-Iran ceasefire and MOU broke down - a timeline
items.content: US-Iran ceasefire struck in April teeters on edge of total collapse. US completed second round of strikes in retaliation. Strait of Hormuz remains flashpoint as Iran demands northern passage control.

items.title: US and Iran threaten return to war after fiercest exchange since truce
items.content: US and Iran engaged in fiercest exchange of fire since April ceasefire. Iran insists ships use northern passage through Strait of Hormuz; US-sponsored southern route near Omani coast breaches MOU terms according to Tehran.

items.title: China AI semiconductor capex could rise 50% annually through 2030
items.content: HSBC Jintrust analyst Chen Ping said AI-focused semiconductor sectors holding up despite weak GDP. Estimates AI capital spending could rise about 50% a year through 2030, boosting China's semiconductor business.
